frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: sensory encoding precision dynamically adapted to ensemble distribution relative to reference

frameworks.name: ensemble coding / robust averaging
frameworks.one_line: items closer to reference weighed more strongly in ensemble average judgment

core_question: How does the relative timing between reference and ensemble stimulus presentation affect efficient ensemble coding?
key_assumptions: encoding precision can be dynamically adapted on a trial-by-trial basis
the reference value shapes the prior used for ensemble encoding
robust averaging reflects efficient allocation of coding resources
manipulations: time interval between ensemble and reference presentation
temporal order: simultaneous vs. ensemble-first vs. reference-first
key_findings: efficient ensemble coding clearly established only when reference and ensemble are simultaneous
efficient coding much weaker when ensemble preceded the reference
efficient coding largely absent when ensemble followed the reference
reduced efficient coding coincided with decreased decision accuracy in asynchronous conditions
interpretations: dynamic efficient ensemble coding requires concurrent access to reference information during encoding
the mechanism underlying robust averaging depends on temporal availability of the reference signal
